mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals monetary tightening that raises discount rates, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns exceed 2% in 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 150k jobs signals robust labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns fall below 0.5% in 70% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k, or t-test p > 0.1 for mean return difference.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction below 2% growth indicates deteriorating economic demand, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns exceed 3% in 50% of instances where GDPC1 declines by >2%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL > 320) and federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.3%) signal stagflationary pressures that depress equity returns through 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns exceed 1.5% in 60% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient < 0.1 in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production below 103.5 signals weakening manufacturing activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns exceed 1% in 70% of instances where INDPRO declines by >0.3%, or t-test p > 0.05 for mean return difference.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer spending (RSAFS > 720k) during inflationary regimes (CPIAUCSL > 320) indicates resilient demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns through 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns fall below 0.8% in 50% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient < 0.15 in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 4.0% signals tight labor market conditions that boost corporate margins, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns fall below 1% in 60% of instances where UNRATE declines by >0.2pp, or t-test p > 0.05 for mean return difference.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST > 1350k) indicate improving demand for construction, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns fall below 0.5% in 70% of instances where HOUST exceeds 1350k, or Rank IC < 0.04 over 2-year windows.
